Q1 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following statement is FALSE?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
The complement of every recursively enumerable sets is recursively enumerable.
Because RE language are not closed under complement.

Q3 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following problems is decidable? (Here, CFG means context free grammar and CFL means context free language)

Correct answer: B, A, C, D
Solution

(b)
(a) We don’t have any standard algorithm to change CFG into CFL from a given CFG deciding a language is finite is decidable but regular its undecidable.
(b) From a given CFG we can determine the CFL and CFL emptiness is decidable.
(c) Intersection of two CFL is undecidable because it is not closed under intersection.
(d) CFL is not closed under Complement so its undecidable.

Q4 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Let M be the 5 stage NFA with ∈-transitions shown in the diagram below:

Which one of the following regular expressions represents the language accepted by M?

Correct answer: B, A, C, D
Solution

(b)
• Counter-example for option (a) : 011 is not generated.
• Counter-example for option (c) : 000 is not generated.
• Counter-example for option (d) : empty string is not generated.

Q5 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which one of the following regular expressions is equivalent to the language accepted by the DFA given below?

Correct answer: A, B, C, D
Solution

(a)
The given DFA accepts all and only binary strings containing odd number of 1’s.
So, option (a) is correct regular expression.
Option (b) Counter-Example: Generates empty string.
Option (c) Counter-Example: Doesn’t generate 10011.
Option (d) Counter-Example: Doesn’t generate 1.

Consider the following PDA, M that accepts all strings having equal number of a’s and b’s. Then for the complete PDA the missing transaction in the diagram are (here z0 is the initial stack symbol)

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Same symbols will be pushed into the stack and opposite symbols will be deleted from the stack.
Hence option (a) is correct.
